title: Chitragupta Chalisa
intro: Shri Chitragupta Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Lord Chitragupta
description:
|| Shri Chitragupta Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Sumir Chitragupta Eesh ko
Satat navaun sheesh
Brahma Vishnu Mahesh sah
Riniha bhaye Jagadish
Karo kripa karivar vadan
Jo Sarshuti sahay
Chitragupta jas vimalyash
Vandan gurupad lay

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Chitragupta gyan ratnakar
Jai Yamesh digant ujagar
Aj sahay avtareu gusain
Kinheu kaaj Brahm kinai
Shrishti srijanhit ajman jancha
Bhanti-bhanti ke jeevan racha
Aj ki rachna manav sandar
Manav mati aj hoi niruttar
Bhae prakat Chitragupta sahai
Dharmadharm gun gyan karai
Racheu dharam dharam jag manhi
Dharam avatar let tum panhi
Aham vivekai tumhi vidhata
Nij satta pa karahin kughata
Shrishti santulan ke tum swami
Tray devan kar shakti samani
Paap mrityu jag mein tum lae
Bhayka bhoot sakal jag chhae
Mahakaal ke tum ho sakshi
Brahmau maran na jaan minakshi
Dharam Krishna tum jag upjayo
Karm kshetra gun gyan karayo
Ram dharam hit jag pagu dhare
Manavgun sadgun ati pyare
Vishnu chakra par tumhi viraje
Palan dharam karam shuchi saaje
Mahadev ke tum tray lochan
Prerakshiv as tandav nartan
Savitri par kripa nirali
Vidyanidhi maa sab jag aali
Rama bhal par kar ati daya
Shrinidhi agam akut agaya
Uma vich shakti shuchi rachyo
Jakebin Shiv shav jag bachyo
Guru Brihaspati sur pati natha
Jake karm gahai tav hatha
Ravan Kans sakal matware
Tav pratap sab sarag sidhare
Pratham pujya Ganpati Mahadeva
Sou karat tumhari seva
Riddhi Siddhi paay dwainari
Vighna haran shubh kaaj sanwari
Vyas chahai rach Ved purana
Ganpati lipibadh hitman thana
Pothi masi shuchi lekhani deenha
Asvar dey jagat krit keenha
Lekhani masi sah kagad kora
Tav pratap aju jagat majhora
Vidya vinay parakram bhari
Tum aadhar jagat aabhari
Dwadas poot jagat as lae
Rashi chakra aadhar suhae
Jas poota tas rashi rachana
Jyotish ketum janak mahana
Tithi lagan hora digdarshan
Chari asht chitransh sudarshan
Rashi nakhat jo jatak dhare
Dharam karam phal tumhi adhare
Ram Krishna guruvar grih jaai
Pratham guru mahima gun gaai
Shri Ganesh tav bandan keena
Karm akarm tumhi aadhina
Devavrit jap tap vrit keenha
Ichha mrityu param var deenha
Dharamheen Saudasa kuraja
Tap tumhar Baikunth viraja
Hari pad deenh dharam Hari nama
Kayath parijan param pitama
Shur shuyashma ban jamata
Kshatriya vipr sakal aadta
Jai Jai Chitragupta gusain
Guruvar guru pad paay sahai
Jo shat path karai chalisa
Janmamaran dukh katai kalesa
Vinay karain kuldeep shuvesha
Rakh pita sam neh hamesha

|| Doha ||
Gyan kalam, masi Saraswati
Ambar hai masipatra
Kaalchakra ki pustika
Sada rakhe dandastra
Paap punya lekha karan
Dharyo chitra swaroop
Shrishti santulan swamisada
Sarag narak kar bhoop
meaning:
|| Shri Chitragupta Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Sumir Chitragupta Eesh ko, satat navaun sheesh.
(Remembering Lord Chitragupta, I constantly bow my head.)
Brahma Vishnu Mahesh sah, riniha bhaye Jagadish.
(Along with Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh, he became the Lord of the world.)
Karo kripa karivar vadan, jo Sarshuti sahay.
(O Lord Ganesha, show your grace, and may Goddess Saraswati help me.)
Chitragupta jas vimalyash, vandan gurupad lay.
(Singing the pure glory of Chitragupta, I bow at the feet of the Guru.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Chitragupta gyan ratnakar.
(Victory to Chitragupta, the ocean of knowledge.)
Jai Yamesh digant ujagar.
(Victory to the Lord of Yama, illuminated in all directions.)
Aj sahay avtareu gusain, kinheu kaaj Brahm kinai.
(You descended to help Brahma and worked like Brahma.)
Shrishti srijanhit ajman jancha, bhanti-bhanti ke jeevan racha.
(For the creation of the universe, you thought and created various lives.)
Aj ki rachna manav sandar, manav mati aj hoi niruttar.
(Among Brahma's creations, humans are beautiful, leaving even the intellect speechless.)
Bhae prakat Chitragupta sahai, dharmadharm gun gyan karai.
(Chitragupta appeared as a helper, teaching the knowledge of virtue and sin.)
Racheu dharam dharam jag manhi, dharam avatar let tum panhi.
(You established virtue in the world; you are the avatar of Dharma.)
Aham vivekai tumhi vidhata, nij satta pa karahin kughata.
(You are the creator and the giver of conscience, destroying evil with your power.)
Shrishti santulan ke tum swami, tray devan kar shakti samani.
(You are the master of cosmic balance, holding the power of the Trinity.)
Paap mrityu jag mein tum lae, bhayka bhoot sakal jag chhae.
(You brought the laws of sin and death, filling the world with fear.)
Mahakaal ke tum ho sakshi, Brahmau maran na jaan minakshi.
(You are the witness to Mahakaal; even Brahma does not know the full mystery of death.)
Dharam Krishna tum jag upjayo, karm kshetra gun gyan karayo.
(You cultivated Dharma in the world and taught the knowledge of Karma.)
Ram dharam hit jag pagu dhare, manavgun sadgun ati pyare.
(Ram descended for Dharma; he loved human virtues deeply.)
Vishnu chakra par tumhi viraje, palan dharam karam shuchi saaje.
(You sit upon Vishnu's chakra, adorning the path of duty and righteous action.)
Mahadev ke tum tray lochan, prerakshiv as tandav nartan.
(You are the third eye of Mahadev, inspiring the dance of Shiva.)
Savitri par kripa nirali, vidyanidhi maa sab jag aali.
(You have a unique grace upon Savitri, the treasure of knowledge.)
Rama bhal par kar ati daya, Shrinidhi agam akut agaya.
(You are merciful to Lakshmi, the mistress of infinite wealth.)
Uma vich shakti shuchi rachyo, jakebin Shiv shav jag bachyo.
(You dwell in the power of Uma; without whom Shiva is but a corpse.)
Guru Brihaspati sur pati natha, jake karm gahai tav hatha.
(Even Jupiter, the Guru of gods, follows your laws of Karma.)
Ravan Kans sakal matware, tav pratap sab sarag sidhare.
(Egoists like Ravan and Kans perished by your might.)
Pratham pujya Ganpati Mahadeva, sou karat tumhari seva.
(Even the first-worshipped Ganpati serves you.)
Riddhi Siddhi paay dwainari, vighna haran shubh kaaj sanwari.
(He has Riddhi and Siddhi, who remove obstacles and ensure auspicious work.)
Vyas chahai rach Ved purana, Ganpati lipibadh hitman thana.
(When Vyas wanted to write the Puranas, Ganpati resolved to transcribe them.)
Pothi masi shuchi lekhani deenha, asvar dey jagat krit keenha.
(You gave him the book, ink, and pen, blessing the creation of the world.)
Lekhani masi sah kagad kora, tav pratap aju jagat majhora.
(With pen, ink, and paper, the world runs by your glory.)
Vidya vinay parakram bhari, tum aadhar jagat aabhari.
(You are full of knowledge and courage; the world is indebted to you.)
Dwadas poot jagat as lae, rashi chakra aadhar suhae.
(You brought twelve sons to the world, the foundation of the zodiac.)
Jas poota tas rashi rachana, jyotish ketum janak mahana.
(As are your sons, so are the signs; you are the great father of astrology.)
Tithi lagan hora digdarshan, chari asht chitransh sudarshan.
(You guide the dates, time, and horoscopes.)
Rashi nakhat jo jatak dhare, dharam karam phal tumhi adhare.
(The fruits of a person's life depend on your cosmic record.)
Ram Krishna guruvar grih jaai, pratham guru mahima gun gaai.
(Even Ram and Krishna went to their Guru's home and sang the Guru's glory.)
Shri Ganesh tav bandan keena, karm akarm tumhi aadhina.
(Ganesh worshipped you, as all actions are under your rule.)
Devavrit jap tap vrit keenha, ichha mrityu param var deenha.
(Bhishma performed penance and was granted the boon of death at will.)
Dharamheen Saudasa kuraja, tap tumhar Baikunth viraja.
(King Saudasa's sins were washed away by your penance, granting him Vaikuntha.)
Hari pad deenh dharam Hari nama, Kayath parijan param pitama.
(You granted the divine abode; you are the great grandfather of the Kayastha lineage.)
Shur shuyashma ban jamata, Kshatriya vipr sakal aadta.
(You are respected by all, Kshatriyas and Brahmins alike.)
Jai Jai Chitragupta gusain, guruvar guru pad paay sahai.
(Victory to you, Lord Chitragupta, please help me like a Guru.)
Jo shat path karai chalisa, janmamaran dukh katai kalesa.
(Whoever recites this Chalisa 100 times, their cycle of birth and death is eased.)
Vinay karain kuldeep shuvesha, rakh pita sam neh hamesha.
(Kuldeep prays to you to keep your affection like a father forever.)

|| Doha ||
Gyan kalam, masi Saraswati, ambar hai masipatra.
(Knowledge is your pen, Saraswati is the ink, and the sky is your inkpot.)
Kaalchakra ki pustika, sada rakhe dandastra.
(You keep the book of time and always hold the weapon of justice.)
Paap punya lekha karan, dharyo chitra swaroop.
(You assumed the form of Chitragupta to record sins and virtues.)
Shrishti santulan swamisada, sarag narak kar bhoop.
(You are the master of cosmic balance and the king of heaven and hell.)

faq.q1: Why is the Chitragupta Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Lord Chitragupta is the deity of justice and the keeper of the records of human karma. This Chalisa is recited to remain conscious of one's deeds, to seek atonement for mistakes, and to walk on the path of righteousness (Dharma).
faq.q2: Should one be afraid of reading the Chitragupta Chalisa?
faq.a2: Absolutely not. Lord Chitragupta is just and fair. Rather than fearing him, remembering him inspires us to distinguish between right and wrong and encourages us to perform our duties with integrity.
faq.q3: What is the most auspicious day to read the Chitragupta Chalisa?
faq.a3: Reciting it on 'Chitragupta Jayanti' or 'Yama Dwitiya' (Bhai Dooj) is extremely rewarding. Apart from this, Thursdays are also considered auspicious for his worship.
faq.q4: Is this Chalisa useful for students?
faq.a4: Yes, Lord Chitragupta is the presiding deity of intellect, writing, and knowledge. For students, reciting this Chalisa is very useful for enhancing memory, focus, and success in academics.
faq.q5: Is there any special ritual to read it?
faq.a5: Chitragupta Ji favors truth and honesty. Instead of complex rituals, reciting this Chalisa with a pure mind, a resolve to be truthful, and full devotion is the best form of worship.
